_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**MONITORING — Assess carrier EV/hydrogen capability on European corridors now, before client commitments on green road transport are made.**

The workspace's carrier network on European lanes will face progressive pressure to operate zero-emission vehicles as the HDV CO₂ standards tighten through 2030 and 2035. AFIR's infrastructure deadlines (hydrogen HRS every 200 km on TEN-T core by end-2030; electric truck charging points at defined intervals on TEN-T core by end-2025 and TEN-T comprehensive by end-2027 — see Section 8) are the preconditions for carrier operational viability. The workspace should now map its European road corridors (particularly those used for live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, and high-value automotive) against published TEN-T network maps and identify where infrastructure gaps currently exist or are expected to persist through 2030.

Action: Request from current European road carriers their published or planned fleet electrification or hydrogen transition timelines and identify which corridors they can currently or imminently serve with zero-emission vehicles. This is a vendor-data exercise, not a compliance exercise. No regulatory deadline applies to the workspace directly. The urgency is commercial: clients are beginning to ask, and the workspace should have sourced answers before it commits to claims in tenders or sustainability reports.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1804, Articles 4–6, EUR-Lex, 22 September 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1804/oj/eng*
